mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decline in unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint movement of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) or the directional relationship reverses in 30%+ of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 2.0% signals inflationary pressures that prompt central bank tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 0.5% indicates robust supply-side activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% but SPX 5d returns are negative in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) above 3% signals strong aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth exceeds 3% but 60d SPX returns are negative in > 30% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low FEDFUNDS and high CPIAUCSL signals stagflationary risks, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) or the directional relationship reverses in 25%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in housing starts (HOUST) above 10k units indicates strong demand for residential construction, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST growth exceeds 10k but SPX 20d returns are negative in > 35% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 5% during full employment regimes signals tight labor markets that boost corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE remains below 5% but 60d SPX returns are negative in > 25% of cases.
